data.headline: Kitchen Remodeling in Darien, CT
data.subheadline: Licensed Connecticut kitchen remodeling contractor serving Darien and Fairfield County — full-service renovations from $30,000 to $150,000+.

data.title: Full-Service Kitchen Remodeling in Darien, CT
data.content:
Kitchen remodeling in Darien typically ranges from $30,000 for a basic cabinet and countertop update to $150,000 or more for a full custom renovation with structural changes. Most projects take six to twelve weeks of active construction, plus cabinet lead time. We hold CT HIC License #0668405 and serve Darien through our Fairfield County Service Area Team.

Darien's housing stock features elegant colonials, Tudors, and classic New England-style homes built primarily from the 1930s through the 1990s. Neighborhoods like Tokeneke, Noroton, and Darien Center have homes with kitchens that were once state-of-the-art but now show their age — dated cabinetry, laminate or tile countertops, and layouts that don't match how families use kitchens today.

Darien homeowners expect clear proposals, daily updates during construction, and a project that finishes on the schedule we quoted. That's exactly how we operate.

data.title: What Is Included in a Kitchen Remodel in Darien
data.content:
A full kitchen remodel in Darien covers every trade from demolition through final paint: cabinetry, countertops, tile, flooring, electrical, plumbing, appliance installation, and all required Darien Building Department permits and inspections.

We start with demolition and removal of existing cabinets, countertops, and flooring. Before any new material goes in, we assess what's behind the walls. Darien homes — especially those built in the 1930s through 1990s — regularly turn up undersized electrical panels, deteriorated plumbing, water damage inside wall cavities, or structural framing that needs attention before the renovation can proceed properly.

Cabinetry installation includes soft-close hardware and adjustable shelving as standard. Countertop fabrication and installation, backsplash tile, and underlayment preparation are all part of the project. Electrical scope covers new circuits for appliances, under-cabinet lighting, and updated outlet and switch locations.

Plumbing includes sink and dishwasher connections, garbage disposal installation, and any relocated water lines. We coordinate appliance delivery and installation so you're not managing that separately.

Interior painting, drywall patching, and daily cleanup are included throughout. We install dust barriers to protect the rest of your home during construction. The project closes with a final walkthrough where we go through every detail together.

data.title: What Makes Kitchen Remodeling in Darien Different
data.content: Darien homeowners prioritize quality and attention to detail. Kitchen renovations here typically involve semi-custom or custom cabinetry, natural stone countertops, and thoughtful lighting design. Many homes have formal dining rooms adjacent to the kitchen, and renovations often involve reconfiguring the relationship between these spaces to create a more open, modern flow while preserving the home's traditional character.

data.title: Darien Building Department and Permits
data.content: The Darien Building Department requires permits for kitchen renovations involving structural, electrical, or plumbing work. The department is efficient but thorough — plans need to meet code precisely. Review times are typically 2-3 weeks. We handle all permit applications and inspection coordination.

data.title: Recent Kitchen Remodeling Projects
data.subtitle: The projects below give you a sense of what we've completed in Connecticut recently.

items.title: Kitchen Remodeling in New Canaan
items.before_text: A New Canaan family wanted to open up a kitchen closed off by a load-bearing wall. We engineered a beam solution, installed white shaker cabinets with a quartz island, and extended hardwood flooring into the new open layout.

quote.text: BuiltWell made it straightforward. Now we can't imagine how we lived before.
quote.author: The Chens

items.title: Kitchen Remodeling in Milford
items.before_text: A Milford homeowner needed to replace dated 1990s oak cabinets and laminate countertops. We removed the peninsula, installed soft-close cabinetry with quartz countertops and subway tile, and extended LVP flooring into the dining room.

quote.text: They showed up when they said they would, cleaned up every day, and the kitchen turned out better than I imagined.
quote.author: Ivana P.

items.title: Kitchen Remodeling in Westport
items.before_text: A Westport couple wanted a brighter, more functional kitchen. We gutted the space, reconfigured the layout to add a center island, installed custom cabinetry with quartz countertops, and added recessed lighting throughout.

quote.text: We cook together every night now. The island changed everything about how we use the kitchen.
quote.author: The Martins

data.subtitle: Kitchen remodeling in Darien ranges from $30,000 for a basic cabinet and countertop refresh to $150,000 or more for a full custom renovation with structural changes.

rows.label: Basic Refresh
rows.notes: Cabinet refacing or stock cabinets, new countertops, updated appliances, paint

rows.label: Mid-Range
rows.notes: New cabinets, stone countertops, new flooring, appliances, updated lighting

rows.label: High-End
rows.notes: Custom or semi-custom cabinets, layout changes, premium appliances, full finishes

data.title: Why Darien Kitchen Remodeling Requires Local Expertise
data.content:
Darien homes present construction conditions that contractors without local experience regularly miss.

Darien homes built in the 1950s through 1970s frequently have original electrical systems that cannot support modern kitchen appliance loads. Galvanized plumbing, undersized panels, and layouts designed for a different era are common in Noroton and older parts of town.

Tokeneke properties near the waterfront may have specific considerations related to coastal proximity. We assess all of these conditions during the initial consultation and build them into the project scope from the start.

data.title: Our Kitchen Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: Every Darien kitchen remodel follows the same five-step process. This structure keeps projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.short: Visit your Darien home or connect by video
steps.description: We visit your Darien home or connect via Google Meet or Zoom to discuss your goals, assess the space, and answer your questions. No charge. No obligation. We note the existing electrical and plumbing conditions upfront.

steps.title: Planning
steps.short: Clear written proposal and line items
steps.description: You receive a clear written proposal covering exactly what's included, how long it will take, and what it costs. We break out cabinetry, countertops, tile, electrical, plumbing, flooring, and Darien Building Department permits separately so you understand exactly where the budget is going.

steps.title: Selections
steps.short: Materials and lead times locked in early
steps.description: We guide you through material choices with options at different price points. We communicate lead times clearly so selections are made on schedule before construction begins. If a material has a long lead time, we flag that immediately.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed schedule. You receive daily updates on progress, a clean job site at the end of every workday, and crews who arrive when scheduled. If something unexpected comes up behind a wall, we contact you that day and present your options.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished project together. We check every cabinet, every drawer, every tile, every light fixture. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete. Your written acceptance at the final walkthrough is the last step.

data.title: Project Timeline
data.subtitle: Most kitchen remodels take ten to twenty-two weeks from signed proposal to final walkthrough.

items.title: Planning & Design
items.description: 1-2 Weeks. Consultation, measurements, material selections, and detailed proposal.

items.title: Cabinet Lead Time
items.description: 4-12 Weeks. Custom orders require the longest lead time. Semi-custom options arrive in four to six weeks.

items.title: Construction
items.description: 6-12 Weeks. Demo, rough-ins, cabinetry, countertops, tile, flooring, and fixtures.

items.title: Final Touches
items.description: 1 Week. Hardware, final connections, punch list, and walkthrough.

items.question: Do I need a permit for kitchen remodeling in Darien?
items.answer: Yes. Most kitchen remodeling projects in Darien require permits from the Darien Building Department, particularly when the work involves electrical, plumbing, or structural modifications. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project; you do not need to manage that process yourself.

items.question: How much does kitchen remodeling cost in Darien?
items.answer: Kitchen remodeling in Darien typically ranges from $30,000 for a basic cabinet and countertop refresh to $150,000 or more for a full custom renovation with structural changes. A mid-range project with new cabinets, stone countertops, new flooring, and updated lighting generally falls between $60,000 and $100,000. Your written proposal includes a detailed cost breakdown by trade so you know exactly where the budget is going.

items.question: How long does a kitchen remodel take in Darien?
items.answer: Active construction on a Darien kitchen remodel takes six to twelve weeks depending on the scope of work. Cabinet lead time needs to be added to that figure: custom cabinets require eight to twelve weeks, semi-custom four to six weeks, and stock cabinets are available within one to two weeks. Your written proposal includes a complete project schedule that accounts for all lead times.

items.question: What types of homes do you remodel kitchens in for Darien?
items.answer: Darien's housing stock is primarily colonials, Tudors, and classic New England-style homes built between the 1930s through 1990s. We're familiar with the construction conditions common in these homes and account for them in every proposal and project scope.

items.question: What is included in a kitchen remodel in Darien?
items.answer: A full-scope kitchen remodel in Darien includes demolition and removal of all existing cabinetry, countertops, and flooring; structural assessment; new cabinetry installation with hardware; countertop fabrication and installation; backsplash tile work; new flooring; all electrical work including new circuits and lighting; all plumbing including sink, dishwasher, and disposal connections; appliance coordination and installation; interior painting; all drywall patching and finish work; Darien Building Department permit applications and inspection coordination; daily cleanup; and a final walkthrough.
